title: Liquidation for ERC721 Collateral Uses Inconsistent Calculations Leading to Undercollateralization
reason: The code repays `possibleRepayAmount` and then computes seized collateral from `actualRepayAmount`; that is internally consistent from the available code. The supported issue here is the `decimals()==0` type check, not a demonstrated under-seizure bug.

title: Implementation Contract Not Validated Before Delegation
reason: This is an admin-only upgradeability trust assumption inherent to the proxy pattern. No permissionless exploit or unexpected privilege bypass is shown.

title: Missing Check for Zero Initial Exchange Rate
reason: The contract already requires `initialExchangeRateMantissa > 0`. Complaining about the absence of an upper bound is an admin-controlled deployment parameter, not a standalone protocol bug.

title: Reserve Factor Can Be Set to 100%
reason: This is an explicit governance-controlled parameter bounded by `reserveFactorMaxMantissa`; it is not a hidden vulnerability in the implementation.

title: Division by Zero in exchangeRateStoredInternal if Total Supply is Zero
reason: `exchangeRateStoredInternal()` explicitly returns `initialExchangeRateMantissa` when `totalSupply == 0`, so the claimed division-by-zero path does not exist.

title: Protocol Seize Share is Hardcoded Without Validation
reason: This is a design choice, not a security issue causing realistic protocol harm.

title: No Maximum Borrow Rate Validation in Interest Rate Model
reason: `accrueInterest()` already reverts if `borrowRateMantissa > borrowRateMaxMantissa`. Installing a malicious model is an admin action, and the runtime check prevents silent over-accrual.

title: ERC20 Transfer Handling May Fail Silently for Certain Tokens
reason: `doTransferOut()` inspects returndata and reverts on failure; it does not silently succeed when the token returns false or behaves non-compliantly.

title: Delegatecall Risk in OErc20Delegator Allows Implementation to Modify Delegator Storage
reason: That is the intended behavior of a delegatecall proxy and does not identify a concrete vulnerability beyond the normal trust model of upgradeable contracts.
